Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Oleosin; modified Cys-OLEOSIN lipid droplet structural protein
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Energy-dense storage oil; biofuel and lipidic bioproduct feedstock
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutively expressed in transgenic fronds under maize ubiquitin promoter; transcript detected in single and combination lines
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Protect factor that modestly increases TAG alone and strongly boosts TAG accumulation when combined with MmDGAT and/or AtWRI1, especially with MmDGAT
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. In tobacco transient expression, intron-containing SiOLE(*) increased TAG more than SiOLE(S). 2. In duckweed, SiOLE(*) alone had little effect on TAG, but with MmDGAT increased TAG to ~3.6% DW in OD lines. 3. In OWD lines, SiOLE(*) contributed to maximal oil droplet formation and TAG accumulation up to 8.7% DW.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; stable overexpression in Lemna japonica; RT-qPCR; TAG/TFA quantification; BODIPY confocal microscopy; synergy analysis
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Continuous light; half-strength SH medium; ambient CO2; 23°C; estradiol induction for W-containing lines
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No major standalone growth penalty reported; in combinations contributing to high TAG, altered lipid composition and chlorosis may occur
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful protect factor for stabilizing lipid droplets and reducing TAG breakdown; best deployed with DGAT and inducible WRI1 in vegetative oil engineering
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Construct included ZmHSP70 intron and outperformed non-intron SiOLE(S) in transient assay; intended to mitigate TAG breakdown from lipid droplets

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Kennedy pathway; terminal TAG assembly step
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an acyl group from acyl-CoA to diacylglycerol to form triacylglycerol
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Energy-dense storage oil; biofuel and lipidic bioproduct feedstock
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutively expressed in transgenic fronds under 2x35S promoter; transcript detected in single and combinatorial lines
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly promotes TAG accumulation and increases total fatty acids when combined with SiOLE(*) and/or CFP-AtWRI1; highest effects in OD and OWD lines
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Single MmDGAT expression increased TAG about 7-fold versus WT/EV to ~0.6% DW. 2. Co-expression with SiOLE(*) caused strong synergy, raising TAG to ~3.6% DW in OD lines. 3. In the OWD combination, MmDGAT contributed to maximal TAG accumulation up to 8.7% DW and TFA up to ~15% DW.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable overexpression in Lemna japonica; RT-qPCR; TAG/TFA quantification by TLC and GC-MS; lipid-droplet imaging by BODIPY confocal microscopy; synergy analysis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control/terminal determinant for TAG accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Continuous light; half-strength SH medium; ambient CO2; 23°C; estradiol induction used only for WRI1-containing lines
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Some combinations containing WRI1 showed growth inhibition; MmDGAT-containing high-oil lines showed altered fatty-acid profiles with reduced C18:3 and chlorosis in high TAG lines
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value pull factor for engineering vegetative oil in duckweed; especially effective when stacked with WRI1 and oleosin; useful cross-kingdom enzyme for boosting TAG synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper notes MmDGAT outperformed tested plant DGATs in prior screening and may avoid plant-specific regulatory feedback; likely favors C18:1/C18:2 incorporation into TAG

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Fatty acid biosynthesis; carbon flux push toward de novo lipid synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Genes associated with conversion of carbohydrates into fatty acids; specific direct targets not identified in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates lipogenic gene expression and increases fatty acid synthesis capacity
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Estradiol via XVE inducible system in transgenic duckweed
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Push factor that increases precursor supply for TAG synthesis, but requires pull/protect partners for maximal TAG accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Energy-dense storage oil; biofuel and lipidic bioproduct feedstock
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Estradiol-inducible in transgenic fronds; higher in W-only lines than in combination lines after induction
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: XVE estradiol-inducible promoter system; XVE driven by Arabidopsis ubiquitin 10 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Induced AtWRI1 increases TAG about 7-fold alone and enhances TFA/TAG strongly when combined with MmDGAT and SiOLE(*), with OWD reaching 8.7% TAG DW
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Constitutive monocot WRI1b expression caused strong TAG increase but severe growth/morphology defects, motivating inducible AtWRI1 use. 2. CFP-AtWRI1 correctly localized to the nucleus in duckweed. 3. Estradiol-induced AtWRI1 alone raised TAG to ~0.6% DW and in OWD enabled maximal TAG/TFA accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable transformation; estradiol induction time course; confocal localization of CFP fusion; RT-qPCR; Western blot; TAG/TFA profiling; growth analysis
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 100 µM estradiol for 4 days; continuous light; half-strength SH medium; ambient CO2; 23°C
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive WRI1b overexpression severely impaired morphology and growth; inducible AtWRI1 reduced this but some W-containing combinations still showed slower growth, chlorosis, and cell death after prolonged induction
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful inducible push factor for vegetative oil engineering; should be conditionally or tissue-specifically expressed to minimize growth penalties; stack with DGAT and oleosin rather than express alone
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CFP-AtWRI1 transcript/protein accumulation was attenuated in some combinatorial lines relative to W alone; high WRI1 levels were not necessary for maximal TAG accumulation
